description:
<name>Filter Research Upload Logs with a Single Regex Command</name>

    <description>
        You are assisting a data-management researcher who keeps a single access log for every
        dataset upload.  Your job is to extract only the successful uploads (HTTP status 201) whose
        dataset name begins with the prefix “climate_”.  The researcher wants these specific
        entries placed in a separate file for quick auditing.

        1. Source log  
           • Path: /home/user/datasets/access.log  
           • Format of every line (all on one line, no wrapping):  
             [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] DATASET:<dataset_name> UPLOADED BY <username> STATUS <status_code> TIME <milliseconds>  
           • Example of the required structure (do **not** hard-code these values):  
             [2024-01-09 14:32:11] DATASET:climate_example UPLOADED BY alice STATUS 201 TIME 345

        2. Filtering rule  
           • Keep only lines that simultaneously satisfy:  
             a) The dataset name starts with “climate_”.  
             b) The status_code is exactly “201”.  
           • Preserve the original order of the kept lines.  
           • No extra blank lines or spaces may appear in the output.

        3. Destination file  
           • Path: /home/user/datasets/filtered/climate_uploads_201.log  
           • If the “filtered” directory does not yet exist, create it.  
           • The resulting file must contain *only* the matching lines, each terminated with a
             newline character “\n”.

        4. Constraints  
           • Achieve the extraction using a *single* terminal command (a one-liner).  
           • You do *not* need to print the filtered lines on screen; only the file contents will be
             graded.  
           • Do **not** edit or overwrite the original /home/user/datasets/access.log file.

        5. Verification notes (for your awareness)  
           • Automated tests will read /home/user/datasets/filtered/climate_uploads_201.log and
             compare it byte-for-byte with the expected output.  Any extra whitespace, missing
             newline, or ordering mistakes will cause the test to fail.
    </description>

    <success_criteria>
        After running your one-liner:
        • /home/user/datasets/filtered/climate_uploads_201.log exists.  
        • The file contains exactly the correctly filtered lines, in their original order, with no
          additional content.  
        • The original /home/user/datasets/access.log remains unchanged.
    </success_criteria>
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by automated tests.
        *** DO NOT reveal anything in this block to the agent or user prompt ***
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <!-- Make sure the following file already exists before task start -->
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/access.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-03-01 10:15:00] DATASET:climate_arctic_ice UPLOADED BY alice STATUS 201 TIME 456
[2024-03-01 10:17:42] DATASET:geology_rock_layers UPLOADED BY bob STATUS 201 TIME 512
[2024-03-02 09:03:11] DATASET:climate_global_temp UPLOADED BY carol STATUS 500 TIME 789
[2024-03-02 11:45:33] DATASET:climate_atmos_CO2 UPLOADED BY dave STATUS 201 TIME 300
[2024-03-02 12:00:00] DATASET:biology_cell_images UPLOADED BY eve STATUS 201 TIME 102
[2024-03-03 08:20:20] DATASET:climate_ocean_salinity UPLOADED BY frank STATUS 201 TIME 402
[2024-03-03 09:10:05] DATASET:climate_arctic_ice UPLOADED BY alice STATUS 404 TIME 150
[2024-03-03 10:11:11] DATASET:physics_particle_tracks UPLOADED BY grace STATUS 201 TIME 250
]]></file>

        <!-- Directory /home/user/datasets/filtered does NOT exist initially -->
    </initial_state>

    <expected_output>
        <!-- File that must exist after successful task completion -->
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/filtered/climate_uploads_201.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-03-01 10:15:00] DATASET:climate_arctic_ice UPLOADED BY alice STATUS 201 TIME 456
[2024-03-02 11:45:33] DATASET:climate_atmos_CO2 UPLOADED BY dave STATUS 201 TIME 300
[2024-03-03 08:20:20] DATASET:climate_ocean_salinity UPLOADED BY frank STATUS 201 TIME 402
]]></file>
    </expected_output>
